title: Frontend CMS admin UI + calendar view wired to canister content and schedule

requirements.summary: Ensure seeded CMS items are visible and editable in the CMS UI and represented with editable dates/description fields
acceptanceCriteria: After deploy, all listed items exist in storage and appear in the CMS UI.
Each seeded item has an English description value (can be placeholder text but must be editable).
Items that only specify month/year still have dates represented in the system (e.g., as a date range within that month/year) and are editable.

file_operations.description: Create a CMS page that fetches and displays all content items (including seeded items) from the canister, and provides edit flows for seeded items so their name/description/dates can be updated. Use shadcn-ui components (tables/cards/dialogs/forms) to present and edit items; verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Add React Query hooks for loading all content items and performing create/update/delete mutations, including cache invalidation so seeded items show immediately after any changes. Use the existing useActor hook to access the canister actor. (React Query usage aligns with core-infrastructure + authorization patterns); verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Create a calendar screen that uses fetched content items and renders seeded items (and all other items) on their effective dates, ensuring month/year-only items still render as a date range entry that can be selected for details. Use shadcn-ui components for layout, filters, and details UI; verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

requirements.summary: Add an authenticated admin/editor CMS UI (CRUD for Events, Scare Zones, Shows, Attractions) including descriptions, type-specific fields, and inherit/override schedule controls
acceptanceCriteria: There is a CMS screen reachable via the existing hash-based routing (same routing style as current pages).
CMS UI supports list + create + edit + delete for all four types.
CMS UI includes controls for: name, description, type-specific fields, and inherit/override dates.
Unauthenticated users cannot perform write operations (UI blocks and backend rejects).

file_operations.description: Wire new hash routes into the existing hash-based routing: add routes for a CMS screen (e.g., #/cms) and ensure it renders instead of the homepage sections when active. Also ensure navigation back to home continues to work with existing behavior.

Add navigation entries for Calendar and CMS using the existing header pattern. For CMS, route via hash navigation (e.g., #/cms) rather than scroll-to-section. Use shadcn-ui Button for nav items; verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Implement CMS UI tabs/sections per content type (Events / Scare Zones / Shows / Attractions) with list + create + edit + delete. Include editable fields: name, English description, useMainHauntSchedule toggle, and date range editor when override is enabled. Include type-specific fields UI matching backend.d.ts (e.g., EventType, ZoneLocation/ScareLevel, PerformanceType, Attraction settings). Use shadcn-ui components (tabs, dialog, form controls, select, switch); verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Add React Query hooks to fetch and update the main haunt operating schedule and to invalidate relevant queries after updates so inherited items reflect schedule changes. Use the existing useActor hook and React Query; verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Add a login/logout button using the existing useInternetIdentity hook; clear React Query cache on logout as recommended by the authorization component docs. Use shadcn-ui Button for styling; verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Implement a first-login profile setup modal (asks for user name) using getCallerUserProfile/saveCallerUserProfile. Show it only after authentication and once profile query is fetched to avoid flashing (per authorization component guidance). Use shadcn-ui Dialog/Input/Button; verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Create an access denied state component for users who are authenticated but lack permission to edit CMS content (e.g., not admin). Provide English guidance to request access. Use shadcn-ui Card/Alert/Button; verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Add React Query hooks to fetch caller role/admin status (getCallerUserRole/isCallerAdmin) and expose convenient booleans for guarding CMS write UI. Use the existing useActor hook + React Query; verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Guard CMS write operations: if not authenticated, show a login prompt (using LoginButton) and disable/hide create/edit/delete actions; if authenticated but unauthorized, show AccessDeniedScreen and keep the page non-destructive. Ensure UI also handles backend authorization errors gracefully (show English error text, do not crash). Use shadcn-ui Alert for errors; verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Add a small 'Main Haunt Schedule' editor section within the CMS page to edit the shared operating date ranges and explain inheritance vs override. Use shadcn-ui components for date range rows and action buttons; verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

requirements.summary: Add a calendar view that displays scheduled items across all content types with filtering and detail viewing
acceptanceCriteria: A calendar screen exists and can be navigated to via hash route.
Calendar shows items on their effective dates (inherited from haunt schedule or overridden per item).
User can filter the calendar by content type (Events / Scare Zones / Shows / Attractions).
Selecting an entry shows its details (name, description, and its type-specific fields).

file_operations.description: Add a route handler for the calendar screen (e.g., #/calendar) using the existing hash-based routing style and render the CalendarPage when active.

Implement calendar UI that (1) fetches content items, (2) computes effective dates (use the item.dates as returned; if the UI needs to show inheritance explicitly, also fetch main schedule), (3) renders items grouped by day/week/month with a type filter, and (4) shows a details panel/dialog on selection including name, English description, and type-specific fields. Use shadcn-ui components for filters, list/calendar layout, and details dialog; verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
Add shared helpers to format dates, labels, and type-specific field summaries for display in calendar and CMS detail panels (English text only).

requirements.summary: Wire CMS lists, details, and calendar to the backend using useActor + React Query with robust error states and cache invalidation
acceptanceCriteria: Frontend uses the existing actor pattern (useActor) and React Query to fetch CMS and calendar data.
Mutations (create/update/delete) invalidate/refetch relevant queries so UI updates without manual refresh.
If the backend is unavailable or returns an error, UI shows an English error state and does not crash.

file_operations.description: Ensure all queries/mutations are implemented with React Query, useActor gating (enabled only when actor is available), and consistent query keys per content type. Mutations must invalidate/refetch the appropriate list/detail queries after create/update/delete.
Implement schedule query/mutation with proper enabled conditions (actor present) and invalidation of any queries that depend on effective dates (calendar + CMS lists).

Replace any hard-coded content with fetched canister data. Implement mutation flows (create/update/delete/update schedule) using React Query mutations, with loading states, success confirmations, and English error states when the canister returns errors.

Load calendar data exclusively from canister queries (content items + schedule as needed) via React Query. Add resilient empty/loading/error states (English) and ensure the screen does not crash on failures.

On logout, clear React Query cached data (queryClient.clear()) so CMS/calendar data does not persist across sessions, following the authorization component guidance; verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
